text_draft:
"Pink Bear!" Susie suddenly grabbed her tight. "We can still hunt!"

Susie ran downstairs, Pink Bear clutched to her chest. In the kitchen, Susie's mom was frosting eggs—colorful ones, hidden in a cardboard box.

"I hid extras inside," Susie's mom said, smiling. "Just in case."

Susie's eyes went wide. Pink Bear felt her own button eyes gleaming.

The hunt wasn't ruined. It was just different now.

Inside. Warm. Still full of possibility.
